title_en: How “Piss Christ” Became a Culture-War Bomb
title_zh: 《“小便基督”如何成为一场文化战争的导火索》

paragraphs.en: The fight over Andres Serrano, Robert Mapplethorpe, and the National Endowment for the Arts became a preview of the politics that followed.
paragraphs.zh: 安德烈斯·塞拉诺、罗伯特·梅普尔索普以及国家艺术基金会之间的争议，后来成了此后政治斗争的预演。

paragraphs.en: “Piss Christ” is a photograph of a crucifix immersed in a container of urine. Why would anyone make such a thing? The customary answer is that it’s social criticism—a commentary on the cheapening of the Christian faith—or a prank, like Marcel Duchamp’s urinal, meant to shock and subvert.
paragraphs.zh: “小便基督”是一张把十字架浸在尿液容器中的照片。为什么有人会拍出这样的东西？惯常的回答是它具有社会批判意味——对基督教信仰被廉价化的一种评论——或是像马塞尔·杜尚那件小便池装置一样的玩笑，旨在冲击和颠覆。

paragraphs.en: But Andres Serrano, the man who made “Piss Christ,” seems to have had none of this in mind. He was doing what Jackson Pollock did when he threw paint on a canvas on the floor, or what Andy Warhol did when he copied the labels of thirty-two Campbell’s soup cans. Pollock wasn’t offering a thesis on the death of easel painting, and Warhol wasn’t satirizing consumer culture. They were just trying to create art.
paragraphs.zh: 但拍摄“《小便基督》”的安德烈斯·塞拉诺似乎根本没想这些。就像杰克逊·波洛克把颜料泼在地面的画布上那样，也像安迪·沃霍尔复印三十二罐坎贝尔汤罐标签那样，他只是在做艺术。波洛克并未想要给出一篇关于立案画衰落的论文，沃霍尔也不是在讽刺消费文化；他们都只是想创作艺术。

paragraphs.en: As an artist, Serrano is largely self-made. Born in 1950 and reared in Williamsburg by a mother who didn’t speak English and was hospitalized with psychotic episodes, he grew up Catholic. A school trip to the Met hooked him; by thirteen, he was visiting the museum on his own. He entered the Brooklyn Museum Art School at seventeen but left after two years and became a drug dealer and an addict in the East Village.
paragraphs.zh: 作为艺术家，塞拉诺基本上是自学成才的。1950年出生、在威廉姆斯堡长大，他的母亲不会说英语，还因精神病发作住过院，但他从小是天主教徒。一次去大都会艺术博物馆的学校参观把他吸引住了；到了十三岁，他就会自己去博物馆了。十七岁时，他进入布鲁克林博物馆艺术学校，却在两年后辍学，转而在东村做毒品贩卖并成为吸毒者。

paragraphs.en: After a decade, he realized that if he didn’t reform he’d never create art, and appears to have willed himself clean. He married the artist Julie Ault, a co-founder of Group Material, a Lower East Side collective producing politically progressive art about subjects such as American intervention in Central America. Artists associated with the group, including Barbara Kruger, Jenny Holzer, and Richard Prince, later became stars. Serrano took up art-making again, exhibiting in small galleries and artist-run spaces like the 4th Street Photo Gallery, a Bowery-area storefront that welcomed minority photographers. He was a downtown artist, and in 1979 downtown artists didn’t make art to make money.
paragraphs.zh: 十年之后，他意识到自己如果不改变，就永远无法继续创作，于是似乎是下定决心让自己“洁净”了。那时他与艺术家朱莉·奥尔特（Julie Ault）结婚，她是“材料小组”（Group Material）的共同创始人，这是一个位于下东城的艺术团体，创作以美国干预中美洲等议题为主题的政治进步艺术。包括芭芭拉·克鲁格（Barbara Kruger）、詹妮·霍泽（Jenny Holzer）和理查德·普林斯（Richard Prince）在内的多位与该团体有关的艺术家后来都成了明星。塞拉诺重新开始从事艺术创作，在小型画廊和由艺术家经营的空间展出，比如“第4街摄影画廊”（4th Street Photo Gallery）——一家位于博伊里区、接纳少数族裔摄影师的店铺。他是地下艺术圈的一员，而在1979年，地下艺术家创作并不是为了挣钱。

paragraphs.en: Convinced he had little talent as a painter or a sculptor, Serrano borrowed a friend’s camera and began taking pictures. He insists he isn’t a photographer, and he doesn’t develop his own prints. He thinks of himself as an artist who uses a camera. His early work was Surrealist-inflected tableaux; the sliced eyeball in Luis Buñuel’s “Un Chien Andalou” has the same mix of coolness and unpleasantness found in a Serrano photograph. He used Cibachrome, a process that yields waxy, saturated color and an illusion of depth. His contemporaries Nan Goldin and Jeff Wall used it.
paragraphs.zh: 他相信自己几乎没有画家或雕塑家的天赋，于是向朋友借了相机并开始拍照。他坚持说自己不是一名摄影师，也不亲自冲洗照片。他认为自己是个“使用相机的艺术家”。他的早期作品是带有超现实主义气质的构图；路易·布努埃尔（Luis Buñuel）的《一条安达卢西亚狗》（Un Chien Andalou）里那只被切开的眼球，拥有塞拉诺作品中同样的冷峻与不适感。他使用了“雪茄光谱转印”——一种会产生蜡质、饱和色彩并有空间错觉的工艺。与他同时代的人物如南·高登（Nan Goldin）和杰夫·沃尔（Jeff Wall）也曾用过这种工艺。

paragraphs.en: Religious iconography was on Serrano’s palette from the start. He collects religious artifacts and uses church pews as living-room couches. Like many nonobservant Catholics, he appears to be anticlerical but not anti-Christian. He isn’t trying to make religious art. He’s trying to make art using religious materials. He treats Christ, the cross, and the Madonna the way Monet treated haystacks: not for or against, just alert to their aesthetic potential.
paragraphs.zh: 宗教意象从一开始就在塞拉诺的创作“色板”里。他收藏宗教文物，并把教堂长椅当客厅沙发使用。像许多不严格实践教义的天主教徒一样，他看上去反对教会权威，但并非反基督教。他并不是在创作宗教艺术，而是在用宗教材料做艺术。他把基督、十字架和圣母玛利亚当作克劳德·莫奈（Claude Monet）对待干草堆那样去看待：既不站在赞成的一边，也不站在反对的一边，只是敏锐地关注它们的审美潜力。

paragraphs.en: In 1985, Serrano was approached by William Olander, a curator at the New Museum of Contemporary Art, which had been founded as a venue for cutting-edge, issue-based art. Olander was planning a group show called “FAKE: A Meditation on Authenticity.” This was a standard topic of postmodernist interrogation. “In a global economy increasingly dominated by high technology capable of reproducing copies more ‘real’ than the real thing,” the catalogue explains, “for a fake to operate as a fake, it must pass as an original, circulating freely in our system of late capitalism.” That kind of thing.
paragraphs.zh: 1985年，塞拉诺受到了纽约当代艺术博物馆（New Museum of Contemporary Art）的馆长威廉·欧兰德（William Olander）的邀请。该馆最初成立时就是一个面向前沿、以议题为核心的艺术场域。欧兰德正在策划一个名为“FAKE：一场关于真实性的冥想”（FAKE: A Meditation on Authenticity）的群展。这正是后现代主义反思的常见议题。正如目录中所写：“在一个高科技不断扩张、并且越来越能制造出比真品更‘真实’的复制品的全球经济中，” “一个伪造品要发挥‘伪造’的作用，就必须以原作的身份通过，能够在我们晚期资本主义的体系中自由流通。” 就是这种思路。

paragraphs.en: That kind of thing was not Serrano’s kind of thing. He isn’t a postmodernist. He makes art for traditional reasons—interesting materials, striking images. But the project gave him an idea: “fake paintings,” or photographs that look like paintings and gesture at famous ones. Among the earliest, made in 1986, was a photograph of two Plexiglas containers side by side, one filled with animal blood, the other with milk; he’d later also use breast milk, menstrual blood, and his own semen. The point was to make bodily fluids read as paint. The result, “Milk, Blood,” was meant to recall Mondrian. He then made monochromes: “Piss,” a container of urine echoing Yves Klein’s International Klein Blue paintings, and “Blut und Boden (Blood and Soil),” alluding to Anselm Kiefer. All these pieces are abstract, and at some point Serrano decided to reintroduce representation into his art. The first such work, made in 1987, was “Piss Christ.”
paragraphs.zh: 这类事情并不在塞拉诺的“牌位”里。他不是后现代主义者；他出于传统原因而作画——有趣的材料、引人注目的图像。但这个项目给了他一个念头：所谓“假画”，即看起来像画、并向名画致意的照片。在最早的尝试之一（1986年）里，他拍摄了两只并排的有机玻璃容器，一只装着动物血，另一只装着牛奶；后来他又会使用母乳、经血和自己的精液。其目的在于让人体液被当作颜料来阅读。由此诞生的《Milk, Blood》本意是向蒙德里安致敬。之后他又做了几幅单色作品：《Piss》，一个装满小便的容器，呼应伊夫·克莱因的“国际克莱因蓝”画作；以及《Blut und Boden（血与土壤）》，影射安塞姆·基弗。所有这些作品都偏抽象，而在某个时刻，塞拉诺决定把再现性元素重新带回作品中。第一个这样的作品是1987年的《Piss Christ》.

paragraphs.en: Serrano seems to have picked the crucifix as something whose significance would be somewhat ambiguous. (A 1988 companion piece, “Piss Discus,” is boring by comparison—it lacks that ambiguity.) “Piss Christ” may also nod to Gauguin’s “The Yellow Christ.” Serrano first tried his wife’s urine, then switched to his own, judging it yellower. He built a two-gallon Plexiglas container himself; the crucifix inside is thirteen inches long, but the photograph is sixty by forty—large enough to function, on a wall, like an altarpiece.
paragraphs.zh: 塞拉诺似乎刻意选择了十字架作为一个意义不那么单一、也更含混的对象。（相比之下，1988年的配套作品《Piss Discus》就显得平淡得多——它缺少了这种含混性。）《Piss Christ》也可能是在向高更的《黄衣基督》致意。塞拉诺先是用了妻子的尿液，之后改用了自己的，觉得后者更偏黄。他亲手做了一个两加仑的有机玻璃容器；容器内的圣像长度十三英寸，而照片尺寸为六十乘四十英寸，大到可以在墙上像一幅祭坛画那样悬挂。

paragraphs.en: As many have noted, without the title no one would object to the image. The urine is transfigured: reddish gold, faintly effervescent, almost tearlike. The critic Wendy Steiner called Serrano’s work “some of the holiest images in contemporary art”—which certainly seems to be on the spectrum of legitimate responses to it. “Piss Christ” isn’t the most sophisticated art work ever made. But it was made by an artist, for artistic reasons.
paragraphs.zh: 正如许多人指出的那样，若没有标题，几乎没人会对这幅图像提出异议。小便被转化为一种状态：呈红金色、微微起泡、几乎像泪珠一样。评论家温迪·斯坦纳（Wendy Steiner）曾称塞拉诺的作品“当代艺术中最神圣的一些形象”——这无疑属于对它的合法回应之一。 《Piss Christ》并非有史以来最复杂的艺术作品，但它是由一位艺术家出于艺术目的创作的。

paragraphs.en: The photograph was first shown, in 1987, as part of an exhibition at the Stux Gallery, on Spring Street, which drew no notice and sold poorly. A year later, the director of the New Museum nominated Serrano for a fellowship administered by the Southeastern Center for Contemporary Art (secca), in Winston-Salem, North Carolina. He was one of ten winners and received fifteen thousand dollars. What happened next is the subject of Isaac Butler’s entertaining new book, “The Perfect Moment: God, Sex, Art, and the Birth of America’s Culture Wars” (Bloomsbury), for “Piss Christ” turned out to be the bomb that set off those culture wars.
paragraphs.zh: 这张照片最初于1987年在纽约春街的斯塔克斯（Stux）画廊展览中展出，未引起关注，且销售不佳。一年后，新博物馆的馆长提名塞拉诺申请由北卡罗来纳州温斯顿-塞勒姆的东南当代艺术中心（southeastern center for contemporary art，简称 secca）管理的研究基金。最终他成为十位获奖者之一，并获得了一万五千美元。接下来发生的事正是艾萨克·巴特勒（Isaac Butler）新书《完美时刻：上帝、性、艺术与美国文化战争的诞生》（Bloomsbury）所讲述的内容，因为《Piss Christ》竟成了点燃那场文化战争的“炸弹”。

paragraphs.en: The term “culture war” derives from the German Kulturkampf, coined in the time of Bismarck for the conflict between the Catholic Church and the Prussian state. It seems to have entered the American vocabulary largely through the sociologist James Davison Hunter’s 1991 book, “Culture Wars: The Struggle to Define America,” and historians have been refining the story ever since: Daniel T. Rodgers’s “Age of Fracture” (2011) takes a wider view, tracing the breakup of older ideas about class, society, history, and collective purpose, while Andrew Hartman’s “A War for the Soul of America” (2015) covers ground closer to Butler’s, framing the culture wars of the eighties as a backlash against the sixties—notably the women’s, civil-rights, and gay-liberation movements. Butler trains his own lens mainly on a few years, from 1989 to 1992, when the National Endowment for the Arts was chaired by John Frohnmayer.
paragraphs.zh: “文化战争”一词源自德语“Kulturkampf”，当时在俾斯麦时期用于指天主教会与普鲁士国家之间的冲突。它似乎主要是通过社会学家詹姆斯·戴维森·亨特（James Davison Hunter）1991年的著作《文化战争：定义美国的斗争》（Culture Wars: The Struggle to Define America）进入美国语境的，从那时起历史学界就一直在不断修正这一叙事：丹尼尔·T·罗杰斯（Daniel T. Rodgers）的《破裂时代》（Age of Fracture，2011）给出了更宽广的视角，追溯了关于阶级、社会、历史与集体目标等旧观念的瓦解；而安德鲁·哈特曼（Andrew Hartman）的《为美国灵魂而战》（A War for the Soul of America，2015）则更接近巴特勒的研究，强调八十年代的文化战争是一场针对六十年代的反击，尤其是对女性主义、民权运动和同性解放运动的反击。巴特勒主要把研究时间聚焦在1989年至1992年这几年，那时国家艺术基金会由约翰·弗罗姆梅耶（John Frohnmayer）担任主席。

paragraphs.en: Frohnmayer was brought in by the Bush Administration to keep what was still a small brush fire from reaching the White House. A lawyer from Oregon who’d chaired the state arts commission and taken First Amendment cases in private practice, he turned out to be too noble for the job. Although he was a Republican—his brother David was Oregon’s attorney general—he’d nonetheless voted for Michael Dukakis in 1988, because, he said, he couldn’t support a ticket with Dan Quayle on it. Which was a pretty good sign he wasn’t ready for the big rodeo. As he wrote in his memoir, from 1993, he believed he “could negotiate anything if people of good will simply got together, discussed the issues, and decided on the course that would most benefit the country.” That might work in Oregon. It does not work on Capitol Hill. Once in office, Frohnmayer got rolled from every direction—congressional enemies of the N.E.A., congressional friends of the N.E.A., the Administration, the art world, even his own staff. He admitted, in his book, that he felt like throwing up every morning before work.
paragraphs.zh: 弗罗姆梅耶是在布什政府邀请下上任的，任务是把这场仍然只是一小团火苗的争议压回到白宫之外。这个出身俄勒冈州的律师曾担任州艺术委员会主席，也曾在私人律师生涯里代理过第一修正案相关案件，但结果证明他对这份工作来说“太有原则”了。尽管他是共和党人——他的哥哥大卫是俄勒冈州总检察长——他仍在1988年投票支持迈克尔·杜卡基斯（Michael Dukakis），因为他说自己不能支持一张把丹·奎尔（Dan Quayle）放在副总统人选上的总统票。这很明显说明他还没有准备好闯上更大的政治场。正如他在1993年的回忆录中写到的，他相信“只要有善意的人聚在一起，讨论问题，并决定出最有利于国家的做法，我想人们可以协商解决一切。”这在俄勒冈也许管用，但在国会山就行不通。上任后，弗罗姆梅耶很快被各方夹击：国会里反对NEA的人、支持NEA的人、行政部门、艺术界，甚至他自己的工作人员。正如他在书中坦承，他早晨上班前每一天都想呕吐。

paragraphs.en: Frohnmayer’s dilemma was that he believed in artistic freedom and didn’t think Congress should dictate what the N.E.A. funded, but he also felt obliged, as a political appointee, to avoid funding work that Congress and the President found offensive. The result was flip-flopping—defending a grant one day, rescinding it the next—with no cover from an Administration he later described as “committed to almost nothing other than continuing to occupy the real estate at 1600 Pennsylvania Avenue.” He knew that, when trouble came, he’d be the first under the bus.
paragraphs.zh: 弗罗姆梅耶的困境在于，他信奉艺术自由，并不认为国会应当决定国家艺术基金会资助什么，但他又认为自己作为政治任命者有责任回避资助那些国会和总统认为冒犯性的作品。结果是摇摆不定——有时为某项拨款辩护，隔天又撤销——而国会山上的行政当局后来更被他形容为“几乎只关心继续占据宾夕法尼亚大道1600号这块地产”。他明白，一旦麻烦来临，自己会是第一个被推下车的人。

paragraphs.en: Amid the Tiananmen Square protests, the fall of the Berlin Wall, and the invasion of Kuwait, Congress was debating whether to defund an agency that consumes less than 0.02 per cent of the federal budget. In retrospect, the culture wars were mostly a distraction.
paragraphs.zh: 就在天安门广场抗议、柏林墙倒塌和科威特遭到入侵的背景下，国会却在讨论是否削减一个消耗不到联邦预算0.02%的机构经费。回头看，这些文化战争大多只是一次转移视线。

paragraphs.en: But, as distractions go, they were highly distracting. The ten SECCA award winners got a travelling exhibition, which arrived, as its final stop, at the state-owned Virginia Museum of Fine Arts, in Richmond. In March, 1989, the Richmond Times-Dispatch ran a letter citing “Piss Christ” and asking whether Christianity had “become fair game in our society for any kind of blasphemy and slander.”
paragraphs.zh: 不过，若论分散注意力，这些做法可谓极具干扰性。十位SECCA获奖作品被组织成巡回展览，最终抵达弗吉尼亚州立美术馆（位于里士满）这最后一站。1989年3月，《里士满时报—新闻报》刊登了一封来信，信中提及《Piss Christ》，并提出了一个问题：基督教是否已经在我们的社会里成了任何形式亵渎与诽谤的“公开战场”。

paragraphs.en: The letter reached Donald Wildmon, the founder of the American Family Association and a longtime decency crusader, and that’s how “Piss Christ” hit the national media. A former Methodist pastor in Mississippi, Wildmon commanded a mailing list of hundreds of thousands of conservative Christians, many of them politicized by fights over school prayer and sex education. Their favored weapon was the boycott. Wildmon had campaigned against the TV show “Three’s Company,” which he considered indecent, and in 1978 he and his followers pressured Sears to stop advertising on “Charlie’s Angels,” which he considered “smut.” In the mid-eighties, he led a two-year boycott that forced 7-Eleven to stop selling Playboy and Penthouse. A photograph by an unknown artist in a regional museum wasn’t obviously configured for collective action; Wildmon’s followers weren’t museumgoers anyway. Then he learned that five thousand dollars of Serrano’s fellowship had come from the N.E.A.—taxpayer money. That changed everything.
paragraphs.zh: 这封信被送到了美国家庭协会创始人、长期的道德整肃活动家唐纳德·怀尔蒙那里，也正是由此让《Piss Christ》登上了全国媒体。怀尔蒙是一位来自密西西比州的前卫斯理教牧师，他掌握着一张数十万人的保守派基督徒邮件名单，其中许多人因学校祈祷与性教育之争而政治化。大家的拿手武器是抵制行动。怀尔蒙曾发起反对电视剧《三人行》，他认为该剧下流不雅；1978年，他与追随者施压西尔斯零售商，使其停止在《查莉天使》上做广告，他认为那是“淫秽内容”。到了80年代中期，他领导了一场长达两年的抵制，迫使7-Eleven停止售卖《花花公子》与《潘趣》杂志。一件出自不知名艺术家在地方博物馆的照片并未自然适合群众性行动；而怀尔蒙的追随者本来也不是美术馆常客。随后他得知塞拉诺的五千美元项目补助来自国家艺术基金会——也就是纳税人的钱。那一发现改变了一切。

paragraphs.en: Wildmon sent materials to the senators and representatives who oversaw the N.E.A., demanding that whoever had chosen Serrano be fired. (In fact, no one there had picked him; grants went through peer-review panels precisely to insulate the agency from political pressure.) Congress couldn’t touch the Virginia museum, but the N.E.A. was its creature. It confirmed the N.E.A.’s chair, held reauthorization hearings, and set its budget.
paragraphs.zh: 怀尔蒙向负责国家艺术基金会（N.E.A.）的参议员和众议员寄送了材料，要求解雇当时选定塞拉诺的人。（实际上，那里并没有任何人“选中”他；拨款是经过同行评审委员会批准的，以便让机构免受政治施压。）国会无法直接干预弗吉尼亚州立美术馆，但国家艺术基金会是国会体制下的“产物”。国会随后召集并确认了N.E.A.主席，举行了续期听证会，并掌控了其预算。

paragraphs.en: On May 18th, Senator Alfonse D’Amato took the floor and called “Piss Christ” a “deplorable, despicable display of vulgarity,” tearing the offending page from the show’s catalogue and throwing it on the floor. Jesse Helms—a longtime N.E.A. antagonist who’d gone after the agency back in 1975 for funding Erica Jong’s novel “Fear of Flying”—rose to agree. Serrano “is not an artist,” Helms said. “He is a jerk.” Four days later, Pat Buchanan cited the work in a Washington Times column titled “Losing the War for America’s Culture?” On May 31st, Senator Slade Gorton made the inventive claim that “Piss Christ” amounted to the sort of “religious argument” the First Amendment barred Congress from funding, and he called for the N.E.A. to cut off SECCA for five years.
paragraphs.zh: 5月18日，参议员阿方斯·达马托登上讲台，称《Piss Christ》是“可憎、下流、令人鄙夷的粗俗陈列”，并撕下展览目录中相关页面后丢在地上。长期反对N.E.A.的杰西·赫尔姆斯接着表示赞同。他说塞拉诺“不是一名艺术家”，并补充道：“他是个混蛋”。四天后，帕特·布坎南在《华盛顿时报》的一篇题为“美国文化之战正在失守？”的专栏中援引了这件作品。5月31日，参议员斯雷德·戈顿提出了一种新说法：认为《Piss Christ》属于第一修正案禁止国会资助的“宗教论辩”类型，并呼吁切断N.E.A.对SECCA的拨款五年。

paragraphs.en: Butler says that SECCA’s director, alarmed by the controversy, asked Serrano whether he could tell reporters the piece was “a protest against the commercialization of religious values.” “It’s not language that I would use,” Serrano replied, “but you could use it.” Since a work’s best defense against critics is that it expresses an idea, this improvised gloss became attached to “Piss Christ” as its get-out-of-jail-free card. Serrano later offered similar explanations. It’s also why the crucifix inside the container is so often said to be made of plastic, a detail implying cheapness and fakery. In fact, Butler tells us, it was made of wood and metal: a real crucifix, owned by a collector who treated religious artifacts as art objects, and not a gift-shop trinket.
paragraphs.zh: 巴特勒说，SECCA 的馆长因争议而担忧，便问塞拉诺是否可以告诉记者这件作品是“对宗教价值观商业化的一次抗议”。塞拉诺回答道：“这不是我会用的措辞，但你可以这么说。”由于一件作品最有力的自我辩护通常是它在表达一个观念，这种临时拼凑出的说法便被贴在《Piss Christ》身上，成了它的“免罪牌”。塞拉诺之后也给出了类似的解释。这也是为什么作品里的十字架常被说成是塑料制的：这暗示了廉价和伪造。实际上，巴特勒告诉我们，它是木头和金属做的：一尊真正的十字架，由一位把宗教器物当作艺术品收藏的藏家拥有，而不是一家礼品店里出售的廉价纪念小摆件。

paragraphs.en: One more shoe had to drop for “Piss Christ” to become more than a political soapbox, and on June 13th it did: the Corcoran Gallery of Art, in Washington, D.C., announced that it was cancelling “The Perfect Moment,” a retrospective of photographs by Robert Mapplethorpe. Unlike Serrano, Mapplethorpe was an established art-world figure—he’d had a major Whitney retrospective—though by then he had died, of AIDS. The show included photographs of gay B.D.S.M. poses (fisting, bondage, water sports) and of partially unclothed prepubescent children. These would be hard to defend as “expressing an idea.” The Corcoran received government money and didn’t want to get pulled into the fight. “With considerable regret,” it announced, the board had voted unanimously to withdraw from hosting the show.
paragraphs.zh: 要让《Piss Christ》从一个政治讲台变成更具冲击力的文化事件，还差最后一把“关键一脚”，而这在6月13日发生了：华盛顿特区的科考兰美术馆宣布取消罗伯特·梅普索普思的摄影回顾展《The Perfect Moment》。与塞拉诺不同，梅普索普思是艺术界的既定人物——他曾举办过一场大型惠特尼回顾展——而且在那时他已经因艾滋病去世。展览中既有描绘同性恋BDSM姿态（包括拳交、捆绑、水上运动）的照片，也有部分未发育青少年半裸的照片。这些内容很难辩称是在“表达一个思想”。科考兰接受了政府资助，不想卷入这场争执。馆方公告说：“遗憾的是”，董事会一致表决撤回举办该展览。

paragraphs.en: The museum thought it was dodging a bullet; it had only painted a target on its back. Artists and arts organizations accused it of censorship. Nine hundred protesters gathered outside the gallery and projected Mapplethorpe’s images onto the façade. The Washington Project for the Arts, an alternative arts organization, picked up “The Perfect Moment,” and it ran there in July and August to record crowds. The Corcoran tried, too late, to get some of the toothpaste back into the tube by asking Group Material to organize a show on censorship which would include works by both Serrano and Mapplethorpe; Group Material refused.
paragraphs.zh: 博物馆当时以为自己是在躲过一场危机；其实它只是给自己的背后贴上了靶子。艺术家和艺术组织随即指责它为审查主义。九百名抗议者聚集在画廊外，并把梅普索普思的影像投射到外立面上。华盛顿替代艺术机构“华盛顿艺术项目”接手了《The Perfect Moment》，并在七月和八月期间在那里展出，吸引了观众。科考兰后来又试图抢救残局，晚了些，转而请 Group Material 主办一场以“审查”为主题的展览，其中包括塞拉诺和梅普索普思的作品；但 Group Material 拒绝了。

paragraphs.en: The art world was not incensed out of love for Robert Mapplethorpe. It was incensed because cancelling the exhibition put every arts organization receiving even indirect government funding on notice. Particularly vulnerable were regional and alternative spaces, which, unlike the Met or MOMA, couldn’t survive without it. As modest as the N.E.A. budget was (it was a hundred and seventy million in 1989 and is two hundred and seven million today), without government money, many smaller parts of the cultural ecosystem would wither and die. The too-big-to-fail museums like MOMA and the Met sailed through the culture wars untouched; from the perspective of the struggling artist, it was not their finest hour.
paragraphs.zh: 艺术界并非出于对罗伯特·梅普索普思的爱而愤怒。它愤怒，是因为展览被取消让所有获得过哪怕间接政府资助的艺术机构都收到警告。尤其是区域性和替代性空间最为脆弱，它们与大都会博物馆或现代艺术博物馆不同，无法在没有政府支持的情况下生存。美国国家艺术基金会的预算虽然不高（1989年是1.7亿美元，如今是2.07亿美元），但没有这类公共资金，文化生态里许多较小的环节都会萎缩甚至消失。像大都会和MOMA这样的“大而不能倒”机构在这场文化战争中轻松度过，但站在困境艺术家角度看，这显然不是对他们最好的时刻。

paragraphs.en: The message of the Corcoran cancellation was that crossing some undefined line of decency gave the government license to come after you. And it did. After “The Perfect Moment” reached Cincinnati’s Contemporary Arts Center, the museum and its director were put on trial for pandering obscenity and using a minor in nudity-oriented material.
paragraphs.zh: 科科兰（Corcoran）撤展的信息是：越过一条模糊不清的体面底线，就等于给了政府以官方名义来围追堵截你的许可。果不其然，确实发生了。在“《The Perfect Moment》”进入辛辛那提当代艺术中心后，博物馆及其馆长被起诉，罪名是煽情地迎合猥亵内容，并在涉及裸露的作品中使用未成年人。

paragraphs.en: Mapplethorpe’s work falls into a few categories: celebrity portraits and self-portraits, flowers, sexual imagery, photographs of the bodybuilder Lisa Lyon, and photographs of the musician Patti Smith. Mapplethorpe met Smith in 1967, when he was twenty, and they were together until 1972—the years she chronicles in her memoir “Just Kids” (2010). He later became part of the gay B.D.S.M. scene he photographed. Smith found that scene off-putting, but the two stayed friends. Some consider the Smith photographs his best work, precisely because they’re the least staged—the cover of her album “Horses” is one of them. His other work can feel formal, even academic. Where Serrano’s figures look waxen, Mapplethorpe’s look like marble. “They are alive but have no life force,” in Butler’s judgment.
paragraphs.zh: 马普尔索普（Mapplethorpe）的作品可分为几类：名人写真和自画像、花卉、性内容照片、健身运动员丽莎·里昂（Lisa Lyon）的摄影，以及音乐人帕蒂·史密斯（Patti Smith）的摄影。马普尔索普1967年与史密斯相识，那时他二十岁；两人一直保持关系到1972年——这正是史密斯在回忆录《Just Kids》（2010）中所记录的那几年。后来他成为了自己拍摄对象——同性恋 B.D.S.M. 圈子的一员。史密斯认为这个圈子令人不适，但他们仍旧是朋友。有些人认为这些史密斯照片是他最好的作品，正因为它们最少经过摆拍——她专辑《Horses》的封面就是其中之一。马普尔索普的其他作品看上去更正式，甚至像课堂范儿。他的画面不像塞拉诺（Serrano）作品中的蜡样人物；在巴特勒（Butler）的评价里，马普尔索普的形象“是活着的，却没有生命力”。

paragraphs.en: The sex pictures of the so-called “X Portfolio” aren’t candids; they’re meticulously composed photographs of men in deliberate sexual poses. For most viewers, they carry a certain sliced-eyeball shock, and Helms toted a black binder of them around the Senate for years, in his campaign against the N.E.A., asking women to leave the room before showing the images to his male colleagues. But Mapplethorpe, a student of photographic history, wasn’t conferring dignity on a taboo subculture; he was after great photographs, although he understood that one of the things that can make a photograph great is the charge of the forbidden. What unified his work, across subjects, was what the French call le glacis de la lumière—a polished, technically exacting finish. (Given that it was the lighting and contrasts that critics admired in his photographs, it’s notable that he didn’t develop his own prints, either.)
paragraphs.zh: 所谓“X 系列”的性图像并非快照式随拍，而是经过精密构图的男性刻意性姿态摄影。对大多数观众而言，它们带有某种一眼就刺破视线的震撼感；赫姆斯（Helms）在其反对国家艺术基金会（N.E.A.）的参议院竞选攻势中，多年来一直把它们装在一个黑色活页夹里，并在向男性同僚展示前先请女性先离开。马普尔索普是一名摄影史研究者，他并非在为某个禁忌亚文化赋予尊严，而是在追求伟大的照片，尽管他明白，使一张照片变伟大的因素之一，是“被禁”的张力。无论题材如何，他的作品之所以统一，是因为法国人所谓的“le glacis de la lumière”——一种光滑、技术上近乎严苛精确的收束质感。值得注意的是，鉴于评论家称赞他照片中光线与对比处理，他并没有自行冲洗、制作自己的照片。

paragraphs.en: That technical emphasis became central to the legal strategy at the Cincinnati trial, where the defense argued that subject matter was beside the point and the art lay entirely in composition. An expert witness described one image of a man urinating into another’s mouth as “a figure study,” and a photograph of finger penetration as “a very ordered, classical composition.” And so on. It worked. The jury acquitted in under two hours.
paragraphs.zh: 这种对技术的强调在辛辛那提审判中成了辩护策略的核心，辩方主张：题材并非关键，艺术完全体现在构图上。一名专家证人把一个“男子对另一个男子口中撒尿”的画面解释为“人体造型研究”，把一张手指插入动作的照片称为“非常有秩序、非常古典的构图”。依此类推，辩护奏效。陪审团在不到两个小时内作出无罪判决。

paragraphs.en: Butler’s book tells many more art-war stories, some involving artists once notorious, like Annie Sprinkle, David Wojnarowicz, and Karen Finley. Finley became known as the performance artist who spread chocolate syrup on her body. But that was the least of it. She simulated defecation and smeared canned yams on her ass while reciting:
paragraphs.zh: 巴特勒的书还讲述了许多更多“艺术之战”的故事，其中一些涉及当年臭名昭著的艺术家，如安妮·斯普林克勒（Annie Sprinkle）、大卫·沃伊纳罗维奇（David Wojnarowicz）和凯伦·芬利（Karen Finley）。芬利以在自己身体上抹巧克力糖浆的行为艺术家而出名，但那只是她“出名”事件里最轻的一桩。她还曾模拟排便，并在朗诵时把罐装地瓜糊在自己屁股上：

paragraphs.en: Let me tell you how I take the yams! I stick it up my granny’s ass! She’s a real, real nice granny! And I’d never touch her snatch, ’cause she’s my granny! Oh and I love her ass, ooh I will take those Belgian waffles, and smear it up her crack.
paragraphs.zh: “让我告诉你我怎么吃这些地瓜吧！我把它塞进我奶奶的屁股里！她真是个非常、非常好的奶奶！我绝不会碰她的那里，因为她是我的奶奶！哦，我爱她的屁股，噢，我要拿那些比利时华夫饼，抹到她的缝里去。”

paragraphs.en: In 1990, Frohnmayer vetoed grants to Finley and three other artists—Holly Hughes, Tim Miller, and John Fleck—and they sued the N.E.A. for viewpoint discrimination.
paragraphs.zh: 1990年，弗罗恩梅耶（Frohnmayer）否决了对芬利和另外三位艺术家——霍利·休斯（Holly Hughes）、蒂姆·米勒（Tim Miller）和约翰·弗莱克（John Fleck）——的拨款，他们随后起诉国家内华达艺术基金会（N.E.A.），指控其实施了观点歧视。

paragraphs.en: Other culture-war skirmishes formed a backdrop to the N.E.A.’s struggles: the removal of Richard Serra’s “Tilted Arc” from Foley Federal Plaza (March, 1989), the Supreme Court’s ruling in Texas v. Johnson that protected flag burning (June, 1989), and the Ayatollah Khomeini’s fatwa against Salman Rushdie for publishing “The Satanic Verses” (February, 1989).
paragraphs.zh: 其他文化战争的冲突也构成了N.E.A.困境的背景：理查德·塞拉（Richard Serra）的《倾斜拱（Tilted Arc）》从弗洛伊联邦广场被移除（1989年3月）、美国最高法院在《德州诉约翰逊》一案中作出的裁决（保护焚烧国旗行为；1989年6月）、以及阿亚图拉·霍梅尼（Ayatollah Khomeini）对萨尔曼·鲁西迪（Salman Rushdie）因出版《撒旦诗篇》而发布的教法谴责（法特瓦；1989年2月）。

paragraphs.en: What were the culture wars about? Their overlap with the Cold War’s end invites the idea that they were a kind of Cold War redux, with the enemy now internal. That was part of the rhetoric, actually, but it wasn’t as if people were thinking, What are we going to do now that the Cold War is over? Hartman’s thesis that they were a post-sixties backlash is hard to dispute, though it doesn’t quite get us to “Piss Christ.” As Butler suggests at points, the N.E.A. fight was a battle less for America’s soul than for the Republican Party’s soul. Attacking the N.E.A. in 1989 meant attacking an agency that Republican Presidents had controlled for seventeen of the previous twenty-one years. Many conservatives who had supported George H. W. Bush when he ran, in 1988, to succeed Ronald Reagan thought that, as President, he had betrayed their cause.
paragraphs.zh: 文化战争到底是关于什么的？它们与冷战结束的重叠让人想到，这是一种“冷战再临”，只是敌人不再在外部而在国内。事实上，这确实是当时话语的一部分，但这并不意味着人们真的在想：冷战结束后我们该怎么办？赫特曼关于它们是六十年代后期反弹的观点很难被否认，尽管这并不能完全把我们带到《Piss Christ》问题上。正如巴特勒在某些地方所指出的，对国家艺术基金会（N.E.A.）的攻击，更多是为共和党党魂而战，而不是为了美国国家灵魂而战。1989年攻击 N.E.A.，实际上是在攻击一个在过去二十一年里有十七年由共和党总统掌控的机构。许多在1988年支持乔治·H·W·布什接替罗纳德·里根参选的人，后来发现自己觉得他当上总统后背叛了他们的事业。

paragraphs.en: Buchanan led that backlash. When he challenged Bush in the 1992 New Hampshire primary, he campaigned, in part, on N.E.A. funding, specifically for “Tongues Untied,” a documentary about the gay Black experience, directed by Marlon Riggs. (As it happened, it was funded during the Reagan Administration, in which Buchanan had served.) Buchanan stunned everyone, including himself, by taking 37.5 per cent of the vote. Two days later, Frohnmayer was fired.
paragraphs.zh: 布坎南领导了这场反弹。当他在1992年新罕布什尔州初选中挑战布什时，他的竞选部分围绕 N.E.A. 经费展开，尤其针对《Tongues Untied》（《束缚之舌》）这部关于黑人同性恋经历的纪录片，这部片子由马龙·里格斯执导。（事实上，这部片子是在里根政府时期获批经费的，而当时布坎南就在政府中任职。）布坎南在这场竞选中拿下了37.5%的选票，连他自己都感到震惊。两天后，弗罗恩梅耶被解雇。

paragraphs.en: Bush was renominated, but Buchanan was awarded a prime-time speech at the Republican National Convention, in August. He delivered a sermon. “There is a religious war going on in this country,” he cried. “It is a cultural war, as critical to the kind of nation we shall be as was the Cold War itself.” Yet the speech was a bridge too far (or maybe just a bridge too soon). In November, Bill Clinton was elected, defeating Bush by almost six million votes, and the temperature around the arts dropped. The fight was now about gays in the military and the Defense of Marriage Act—just as heated, but more consequential.
paragraphs.zh: 布什赢得了提名，但布坎南在8月的共和党全国大会上获得了黄金时段发言机会。他发表了一场近乎讲道式的演说：“这国家正在经历一场宗教战争。”他喊道，“这是一场文化战争，关系到我们将成为什么样国家的重要程度，正如冷战本身所具有的关键性。”然而这次演讲要么是说得太过了，要么是时机为时过早。11月，克林顿当选总统，以接近六百万票的优势击败布什，围绕艺术的话题逐渐降温。斗争转向了军中同性恋者与《婚姻保护法案》，同样激烈，却更具深远影响。

paragraphs.en: In 1994, the N.E.A. largely ended grants to individual artists and sharply restricted sub-granting, the mechanism that had let SECCA pass money to Serrano. In 1998, the Supreme Court, in National Endowment for the Arts v. Finley, upheld a congressional requirement that N.E.A. grants weigh “general standards of decency” alongside “artistic excellence and artistic merit.” Justice David Souter was the lone dissenter. He argued that indecency is itself a viewpoint, the viewpoint of people who reject society’s definition of decency, and thus protected speech.
paragraphs.zh: 1994年，N.E.A. 基本停止向个人艺术家发放资助，并大幅收紧分拨经费这一机制，而该机制曾让南佛罗里达大学艺术中心（SECCA）将钱交给塞拉诺。1998年，美国最高法院在“国家艺术基金会诉芬利案”（National Endowment for the Arts v. Finley）中支持国会的要求，认为 N.E.A. 的资助应当在“艺术卓越性”和“艺术价值”之外，同时考虑“社会一般体面标准”。戴维·萨瑟是唯一的反对票。他认为“猥亵”本身就是一种观点，是那些拒绝社会对体面定义的人的观点，因此应受言论自由保护。

paragraphs.en: One thing that’s odd in this story is that Frohnmayer never emulated his counterpart at the National Endowment for the Humanities, Lynne Cheney, who routinely vetoed grants she disliked and faced no reauthorization fights or lawsuits. (Being married to the Secretary of Defense probably didn’t hurt.) As an agency head, Frohnmayer could have done the same; his decisions, like hers, would have been effectively unreviewable. Instead, he boxed himself in. He made enemies of artists without satisfying anyone else, though pleasing artists was never the job.
paragraphs.zh: 这个故事里有一点很怪异：弗洛姆迈尔（Frohnmayer）并没有效仿其在美国国家人文基金会（National Endowment for the Humanities）中的同僚琳·陈尼（Lynne Cheney）的做法，后者会对不喜欢的拨款案例行否决，而且并未遭遇续期争论或诉讼（和国防部长结婚可能也没有造成坏处）。作为机构负责人，弗洛姆迈尔完全可以这么做；和她一样，他的决定基本上也不可能被复核。可他却把自己置于困境之中。他得罪了艺术家，却没有真正讨好任何人，而且“取悦艺术家”本来就不该是他的职责。

paragraphs.en: Should the government have funded individual artists at all? When the two endowments were created, in 1965, the arts were still largely understood in formalist, apolitical terms. “Artistic excellence” made sense as a standard independent of content. By 1975, that consensus was gone. When the culture wars broke out, contemporary art was already politicized, as it remains. In some places, “excellence” was considered an ideologically loaded concept.
paragraphs.zh: 政府是否该给个别艺术家提供资助？当这两家基金会在1965年成立时，艺术仍主要以形式主义、非政治化的方式被理解。“艺术卓越”作为一种脱离内容的标准是有意义的。到1975年，这种共识已经消失。等到“文化战争”爆发时，当代艺术早已政治化，至今仍然如此。在某些场域里，“卓越”被视为一个带有意识形态色彩的概念。

paragraphs.en: Serrano and Mapplethorpe were, by the standards of the moment, fairly aesthetically motivated, but their work could be given, and was given, political interpretations. The critical animus was heightened in the late eighties by the AIDS crisis, when many in the art world felt that the Reagan Administration had turned its back on what was happening. Artists thus found themselves in the position of complaining that they were being censored by a government they were attacking.
paragraphs.zh: 塞拉诺（Andres Serrano）和马普尔索普（Mapplethorpe）按当时标准来看，都算是相当偏美学取向的艺术家，但他们的作品可以被赋予，也确实被赋予了政治化解读。到了八十年代末，随着艾滋病危机的出现，这种批评性敌意进一步加剧；当时艺术界许多人都觉得里根政府已经对正在发生的事情背弃了支持。于是艺术家们成了这么一种尴尬局面：他们一边抨击政府，一边却又抱怨自己遭到这个政府的审查。

paragraphs.en: Butler’s book is fair but not disinterested, as the author makes clear. He casts himself as a champion of creative freedom, opposed to any content restrictions that Congress might impose on the N.E.A. He is on the side of Andres Serrano and Karen Finley: if their work was recommended by a peer-review process, no matter how offensive it might be to some, it should have been funded. He gives no quarter to Donald Wildmon, Pat Buchanan, or even George H. W. Bush. He is sympathetic to Frohnmayer, but only when Frohnmayer is standing up to the White House.
paragraphs.zh: 巴特勒的这本书公允却并非“超然”。作者写得很明确：他把自己定位为创造自由的倡导者，反对国会可能对国家艺术基金会（N.E.A.）施加的任何内容限制。他站在安德烈斯·塞拉诺（Andres Serrano）和卡伦·芬利（Karen Finley）一边：只要他们的作品通过了同行评审程序，即使对某些人来说冒犯性很强，也应该获得资助。他对唐纳德·怀尔蒙（Donald Wildmon）、帕特·布坎南（Pat Buchanan）甚至乔治·H·W·布什（George H. W. Bush）都不留情。 他对弗洛姆迈尔表示同情，但只是在弗洛姆迈尔顶着白宫压力时。

paragraphs.en: The war within the Republican Party was not principally an ideological war, though. It was a class war. A patrician like Bush was educated to accept that advanced art and literature could be offensive. Being offensive was its social role, harmless as long as the art was safely ensconced in a museum. If you didn’t like the art, you didn’t go to the museum. At one point, as things were going off the rails at the N.E.A., Bush wrote a note to Frohnmayer: “I do support you—difficult job. But Barbara and I are bothered about the raunchy stuff—urine... fist up rectum.” That’s a conversation I would love to have overheard.
paragraphs.zh: 共和党内部的这场战争并不主要是意识形态之争。它更像是一次阶级之争。像布什这样的贵族出身者受过教育，知道所谓高深的艺术和文学可以带有冒犯性：冒犯是它的社会角色，只要艺术被安全地放在博物馆里，这种冒犯就算无害。如果你不喜欢这件艺术，就别去博物馆看它。当国家艺术基金会（N.E.A.）的局势一度失控时，布什给弗罗恩马耶写过一封便条：“我确实支持你——这份工作很艰难。但芭芭拉和我都对那种猥亵的内容感到困扰——尿液……肛门里伸出的拳头。”这正是我很想“听到”的一段对话。

paragraphs.en: But for Wildmon and Buchanan, in-your-face avant-garde art was an insult to the average person, certainly the average American Christian. Those people did not want to pay for it, and it was their right to object, as one might object to one’s taxes being used to build a triumphal arch or to government funding of white-supremacist speech. In America, it is O.K. to object. If we stopped having these “wars,” we would be less of a democracy.
paragraphs.zh: 但对怀德蒙和布坎南来说，露骨的先锋艺术对普通人，尤其是美国普通基督徒来说，是一种侮辱。这些人不想为此买单，而他们有权反对；就像有人有权反对自己的税款被用于修建凯旋门，或反对政府资助的白人至上主义言论一样。在美国，表达反对是被允许的。如果我们不再有这些“战争”，我们的民主程度反而会降低。

paragraphs.en: What is slightly absurd about the N.E.A. culture wars is the amount of ammunition expended over a tiny amount of money and a tiny amount of art work. Individual grants were always a sliver of N.E.A. spending. Most of the agency’s money supports local and state cultural infrastructure. Butler tells us that, in the agency’s first thirty-five years, between 1965 and 2000, the number of local arts councils grew from four hundred to four thousand, nonprofit theatres from fifty-six to three hundred and forty, symphony orchestras from nine hundred and eighty to eighteen hundred, and dance companies eighteenfold. At roughly two hundred million dollars a year, that’s a modest price to pay for spreading the arts beyond the coasts, and a lot to give up over a few photographs. The German government spends 2.57 billion euros a year on the arts.
paragraphs.zh: 国家艺术基金会“文化战争”中最可笑的一点在于：为了极小的一笔资金和极少量的艺术作品，消耗了大量的火力。单个项目拨款一直只是基金会开支的一小部分。该机构的大部分经费都用于支持地方和州级文化基础设施。巴特勒告诉我们，在该机构成立后的前三十五年（1965年至2000年）里，当地艺术委员会的数量从四百家增长到四千家，非营利剧院从五十六家增长到三百四十家，交响乐团从九百八十个增长到一千八百个，舞蹈团更是增长了十八倍。按每年约两亿美元来算，这只是把艺术带到海岸线以外地区的合理成本，却是为了几张照片而放弃的一笔不小代价。德国政府每年在艺术上投入25.7亿欧元。

paragraphs.en: Today, does anyone really care about “raunchy stuff” in art museums? Or anywhere? If you want raunch, you just open your browser or turn on HBO. A screaming match over a photograph of a crucifix immersed in urine seems almost quaint. ♦
paragraphs.zh: 今天，真的有人还在意艺术馆里的“猥亵内容”吗？或者在任何地方都在意吗？如果你想看露骨内容，你只需打开浏览器或打开HBO。一场围绕“十字架浸在尿液中的照片”的尖叫式争吵，听上去几乎有点过时了。♦
